LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- PLAINTIFF MARVELL SEMICONDUCTOR, INC., A CALIFORNIA, MARVELL INTERNATIONAL, LTD., A BERMUDA CORPORATION, MARVELL ASIA PTE LTD., A SINGAPORE CORPORATION'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR REDACTION OF PUBLICLY AVAILABLE DOCUMENTS IS DENIED IN SO FAR AS THE PLAINTIFF HAS SOUGHT TO HAVE PERMISSION TO FILE REDACTED DOCUMENTS IN THE FORM THEY PROPOSED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CARY ITS BURDEN THAT FACTS WHICH ARE AT THE CORE OF ITS COMPLAINT SHOULD BE FILED UNDER SEAL. LODGED DOCUMENTS WILL BE RETURNED TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS. THE COURT DECIDED THAT THE COMPLAINT MAY BE IN SAME FORM AS EXISTING COMPLAINT EXCEPT IT WILL IDENTIFY CUSTOMERS BY FICTITIOUS NAMES. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL REQUEST FOR AN IN-CAMERA HEARING WAS DENIED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION OVER THE CLAIM FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, EVEN IF THE PRAYER SEEKS RELIEF THAT IS NOT ULTIMATELY AVAILABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFFS MARVELL SEMICONDUCTOR, INC., MARVELL INTERNATIONAL, LTD., MARVELL ASIA PTE LTD.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED AS A MATTER OF FUNDAMENTAL COMITY. THIS COURT CANNOT DISQUALIFY AN ATTORNEY FROM APPEARING IN A DIFFERENT MATTER PENDING BEFORE A FEDERAL COURT IN ANOTHER STATE (SEE IN RE COMPLEX ASBESTOS LITIGATION (1991)232 CAL. APP. 3RD 572, 584-85). THIS RESULT IS NOT CHANGED BY THE PLAINTIFF SEEKING RELIEF IN THE FORM OF AN INJUNCTION RATHER THAN DISQUALIFICATION. PLAINTIFF HAS A SPEEDY AND EFFECTIVE REMEDY AT LAW BY PROCEEDING DIRECTLY IN THE TEXAS ACTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

ATTY. GUY D. CALLADINE OF CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT TOWNSEND& TOWNSEND AND CREW LLP.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PLAINTIFF MARVELL SEMICONDUCTOR, INC., MARVELL INTERNATIONAL AND MARVELL ASIA PTE LTD.- Motion To Maintain Confidentially Of Defendants Opposition Papers To Plaintiff's Motion For Preliminary Injunction IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT THE NAMES OR IDENTIFICATION OF CUSTOMERS A AND B SHOULD REMAIN UNDER SEAL AND SHOULD BE REDACTED EFFECTIVELY. IT IS LIKEWISE GRANTED AS TO PROVISIONS OF PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT OTHER THAN THOSE PROVISIONS THAT DIRECTLY GIVE RISE TO A RIGHT TO INDEMNITY OTHERWISE DENIED AS TO OTHER DOCUMENTS. THE ORDER SHOULD INCLUDE A DIRECTION TO THE CLERK TO RETURN THE UNREDACTED VERSION AND SUBSTITUTE THE REDACTED VERSION. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189 =301/PJB/PM

Notice Of Motion Andc Motion For Redaction Of Publicly Available Documents
Off Calendar Apr-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Jeff Nussbaum, Esq. of Liner, Yankelevitz, Sunshine & Regenstreif, 415-489-7700 for plaintiff and moving party Marvel Semiconductor, Inc.
Guy Calladine, Esq. of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson, 415-391-8140 for defendant and opposing party Townsend & Townsend & Crew

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows --
PLAINTIFF MARVELL SEMICONDUCTOR, INC., A CALIFORNIA, MARVELL INTERNATIONAL, LTD., A BERMUDA CORPORATION, MARVELL ASIA PTE LTD., A SINGAPORE CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion For Redaction Of Publicly Available Documents IS DENIED IN SO FAR AS THE PLAINTIFF HAS SOUGHT TO HAVE PERMISSION TO FILE REDACTED DOCUMENTS IN THE FORM THEY PROPOSED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CARY ITS BURDEN THAT FACTS WHICH ARE AT THE CORE OF ITS COMPLAINT SHOULD BE FILED UNDER SEAL. LODGED DOCUMENTS WILL BE RETURNED TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS. THE COURT DECIDED THAT THE COMPLAINT MAY BE IN SAME FORM AS EXISTING COMPLAINT EXCEPT IT WILL IDENTIFY CUSTOMERS BY FICTITIOUS NAMES. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL REQUEST FOR AN IN-CAMERA HEARING WAS DENIED. (301/PJB/PM)
Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189
